description_long: This object is a rectangular solar energy installation featuring a large array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a raised platform. The main section consists of two distinct groups of solar panels, each tilted at an angle to maximize sunlight exposure. The panels are supported by a robust, dark-colored frame that appears to be constructed from metal, likely steel or aluminum, and is set atop a broad, flat base that may be concrete or another heavy material. The installation is surrounded by a low perimeter wall, creating a contained area that may house additional electrical equipment or provide structural support. The surface beneath and around the installation is paved, suggesting placement in an urban or semi-urban environment. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent, and the structure is clearly designed to be a fixed, non-movable part of the built environment.
description: A rectangular solar energy installation with two groups of tilted photovoltaic panels on a raised, walled platform, set on a paved surface. The structure is robust, metallic, and designed for fixed urban deployment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the installation, with both groups of solar panels clearly visible and the perimeter wall enclosing the platform.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the platform, with the paved base extending beyond the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the back of the installation, the supporting frame, and the open area within the perimeter wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angled orientation of the panels and the structural supports beneath them.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing one direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a raised platform with a metal (likely steel or aluminum) frame.
attribute_array_configuration: Two groups of panels arranged in parallel rows, each group containing approximately 6-8 panels in a linear configuration.
